_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Duraplasty/ : Duraplasty_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Duraplasty_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Presence of Chiari malformation requiring foramen magnum decompression
Symptomatic CM-I with lower cranial nerve palsies, syringomyelia, myelopathy, cerebellar symptoms, severe neck pain, or occipital headache
CM-II and CM-III requiring decompression of tight posterior fossa structures

preauth.label: Clinical notes with signs, symptoms, indications, planned line of management and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and imaging not indicative of surgery at pre-authorization
Imaging did not confirm the diagnosis at pre-authorization
MRI Brain and Spine report missing at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are not available
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are not available
Post-op CT CVJ (craniovertebral junction) not submitted
Discharge summary is missing or does not include follow-up advice

clinical_key_pointers: The primary indication for foramen magnum decompression is the presence of Chiari malformation. The operation has been historically considered to be the primary and gold standard operation for this condition.
surgical_options: Posterior decompression via suboccipital craniectomy with or without duraplasty.
Anterior decompression of the foramen magnum by odontoidectomy and shunting.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY
contraindications: Asymptomatic patients with an incidental diagnosis of CM-I who do not have syringomyelia can be managed conservatively with clinical and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) surveillance.
